name: Haskell National Bank
description_short: Community bank with two Texas locations offering personal and business banking, loans, and deposit products to rural and small-town customers.
description_long:
Haskell National Bank is a community-focused financial institution operating two branches in Texas: one in Haskell (established as a regional bank serving rural areas) and one in Abilene. The bank positions itself as a relationship-based lender serving agriculture, construction, and small business communities in West Texas.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, CDs, IRAs, and various loan products (mortgages, construction loans, livestock and equipment loans, operating loans). Business customers access dedicated checking, money market accounts, and business lending. Additional services include online and mobile banking, credit cards, wire transfers, safe deposit boxes, and financial calculators.

Haskell National Bank differentiates itself through specialization in agricultural and construction lending, which aligns with the rural Texas economy. The bank emphasizes personal service with dedicated phone lines for each location and maintains a community-oriented approach, as evidenced by links to local organizations and Community Reinvestment Act disclosures. Their fee structure is transparent and published online.

As a small regional bank, Haskell National Bank is best suited for customers in their service areas seeking relationship-based banking rather than digital-first experiences. Customers should note the limited branch network (two locations only) and the moderately high fees for certain services (NSF charges of $35, overdraft rates at 18% annually). The bank appears financially sound but lacks the technology infrastructure or national presence of larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Specialization in agricultural lending (livestock, equipment, operating loans) tailored to rural Texas economy
Free incoming wire transfers for customers; reasonable $25 outgoing wire fee
Transparent, published fee schedule available online for comparison
Personal relationship banking with dedicated local phone numbers for each branch
Construction and equipment financing options explicitly marketed and available
Safe deposit boxes available at reasonable rates ($10-$50 annually depending on size)
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only two branch locations in Texas (Haskell and Abilene)
High NSF (non-sufficient funds) fee of $35 per item and 18% annual overdraft balance charge
Account closure fee of $25 if closed within 90 days may discourage short-term customers
No mention of competitive interest rates or yield information on savings/CD products
IRA transfer fee of $25 and early IRA closure fee of $50 are restrictive for account flexibility
best_for: Agricultural and ranching businesses in West Texas seeking specialized lending expertise
Construction companies needing construction loans and equipment financing
Small business owners in Haskell and Abilene areas wanting relationship-based banking
Rural residential customers seeking mortgage and personal lending in their local communities
diagnosis: Haskell National Bank is best for small business owners, farmers, ranchers, and rural residents in the Haskell and Abilene, Texas areas seeking specialized agricultural and construction lending with personal service. The main caveat is the limited branch network and geographic service area—this bank is not suitable for customers outside West Texas or those seeking national banking reach or digital-first services.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
